title: Incorporated Building Information Modeling and the Foundations Data Theory Strategy to Create a Sustainable Construction Industry
author: Mehrab Hemmati Farahani
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Iran is one of the wealthiest countries in the world to have renewable and clean energies, with its climatic diversity and enormous size. With proper development, these sustainable energy sources have the potential to contribute significantly to the growth of renewable energies. It is essential to achieve the goals of sustainable development. This article examines the results of using the sustainable development model in the construction industry to reduce construction's environmental, economic, and social effects. In addition, this includes achieving energy efficiency use, optimization, accurate timing, cost reduction, and increasing safety in construction. This city has a high potential for building information modeling to develop a sustainable construction industry. The information was gathered using mixed research methods (qualitative and quantitative). The primary method used was to interview experts, professors, and specialists involved in construction projects in the municipality of the 3rd district of Tehran. Applying renewable energy technologies in the construction sector requires technical solutions from stakeholders, such as building owners, real estate developers, and energy producers. The results of the research showed that The lack of a comprehensive and codified national program with quantitative criteria that is legally established is one of the important reasons for the non-development of non-acceptable energies in Iran and the consequences of these dimensions will cause the non-sustainable development of the construction industry.
